ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-04-2012 IN DEPT. 503. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PER CCP 36(A) AND (D). TRIAL WILL BE SET EARLIER THAN 120 DAYS CONDITIONED ON PLAINTIFF'S AGREEMENT THAT SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE HEARD ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. TRIAL IS JULY 9, 2012 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS JUNE 28, WITH OPPOSITIONS DUE 5 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING AND REPLIES DUE 2 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. PLAINTIFF TO MAKE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN JUNE 11, 2012. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 9:00 A.M. ON JUNE 28, 2012. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS JUNE 22, 2012. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF IS JULY 6. NO LATER THAN APRIL 27, 2012, PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT, THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. NO LATER THAN APRIL 27, 2012, DEFENDANTS MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. ANY PERSON NOT IDENTIFIED ON THE STATEMENTS WILL BE BARRED FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL OR PRESENTING A DECLARATION ON A SUMMARY MOTION ABSENT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JUNE 27, 2012 AT 1:30PM IN DEPT. 503. A FURTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH JUDGE JACKSON WILL BE HELD ON JUNE 29, 2012 AT 10:00 A.M. ALL COUNSEL WITH THE REMAINING PARTIES / DECISION MAKERS HAVE TO BE PRESENT WITH AUTHORITIES TO SETTLE. ALL PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENTS TO THE COURT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William J. Goines of GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP (650-328-8500), appearing for defendants / moving parties Lorillard Tobacco Company and Hollingsworth & Vose Company
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Melvin Desin

RULING:
DEFENDANT HOLLINGSWORTH & VOSE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Mr. Goines informed the Court that he would file the Substitution of Attorney for defendant Hollingsworth today. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hollingsworth & Vose Company's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's fraud/deceit cause of action and claim for punitive damages are denied. On all issues, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all of plaintiff's evidence in support of his claims against defendant. Defendant also failed to submit affirmative evidence which negates an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Defendant may not incorporate and adopt another defendant's motion for summary judgment/adjudication. (See Frazee v. Seely (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 627.) Order signed in open court. Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William J. Goines of GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP (650-328-8500), appearing for defendants / moving parties Lorillard Tobacco Company and Hollingsworth & Vose Company
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Melvin Desin

RULING:
DEFENDANT LORILLARD TOBACCO COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Lorillard Tobacco Company's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's fraud/deceit - Kent cause of action (issue 1) and claim for punitive damages (issue 2) are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden of production via the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. The court considered the entirety of Dr. Gibbs' declaration. Defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and Dr. Hammar's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his developing mesothelioma. Defendant's objections to Dr. Hammar's declaration are overruled. As to adjudication of the fraud/deceit cause of action and plaintiff's claim for punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's attempt to use its own interrogatory responses are sustained. Order signed in open court. Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

RULING:

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.'s amended motion for reconsideration of the court's order granting plaintiff's motion for preferential trial setting is granted. Defendant demonstrated new facts as required by C.C.P. 1008 (a) - Dr. Yom's deposition, which occurred subsequent to this court's ruling on plaintiff's preference motion.
